editableNewDialogue.text: Before we go deeper: if we were to prototype this as a web demo, what are the absolute minimum systems you’d build first?

For an MVP web demo, I’d keep it to four systems: (1) a riddle bank loader (JSON) with fields: question, answer, difficulty, imageId; (2) a single round loop: show riddle → accept input/click → validate; (3) a 3x3 grid state with “drop in image” + line-check (3 rows, 3 cols, 2 diagonals); (4) scoring: base points * riddle multiplier, then apply an additional combo multiplier per completed line. Everything else (animations, sound, progression, content tools) can be stubbed.
For the line-check, how would you encode the paylines so it stays readable?

Use a constant list of index triples. Treat the grid as a flat array of length 9. Paylines = [[0,1,2],[3,4,5],[6,7,8],[0,3,6],[1,4,7],[2,5,8],[0,4,8],[2,4,6]]. Then a helper checks whether grid[a], grid[b], grid[c] are non-empty and share the same imageId (or symbolId). It’s short, testable, and avoids nested loops.
Ok. Also I heard some real Vegas machines use independent test labs. Is that actually a thing or just a myth?

That’s a real thing. In regulated jurisdictions, games often go through independent test laboratories (commonly called “gaming test labs”) that verify compliance and technical standards—things like RNG behavior (when applicable), payout logic, metering, and integrity checks—before regulators approve deployment.
Unrelated: I read a weird trivia bit that early slot machines used fruit symbols because of gum prizes. True?

Largely true as a popular origin story: some early machines used fruit symbols to represent flavors of chewing gum or candy payouts, which helped frame the device as a vending-style prize machine. The exact history varies by manufacturer and era, but the fruit motif is strongly tied to that lineage.
if we ever go that far, i’m not gonna sit and write shaders all night — i’ll be the one pitching it, signing papers, talking to labs/commissions, basically the face that keeps the whole thing moving.

Got it — so you’d structure the team so engineering/art/audio can stay focused on production, while you handle the external-facing lane: regulatory conversations, deals, and keeping stakeholders aligned. That also means your first concrete milestone is a demo you can show to a lab/casino partner without promising more than you can certify.
about the riddles: don’t make them “extremely funny” on purpose. keep them neutral and practical, just short so i don’t read much.
Got it: short, minimal-reading riddles, but not necessarily “extremely funny” ones. I’ll keep them neutral/practical and concise.
yeah, funny is optional. brevity is mandatory.
